titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Collection of Bookplates by Stephen Gooden
titlestmt.author: Compiled by Nathaniel Dolquist

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 7, 2018 at 00:05

did.unittitle: Collection of bookplates by Stephen Gooden
did.abstract: The collection contains bookplates designed by Stephen Gooden (1892-1955). It also includes process materials—e.g., original sketches, trial proofs, and engraved plates—as well as correspondence and ephemera.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Yale Bookplate Collection was formerly housed in Sterling Memorial Library. The collection was incorporated into the Arts of the Book Collection in 1984 and then moved to the Seeley G. Mudd Library in 1990. In 2011, it was transferred to the Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright has not been transferred to Yale University.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Collection of Bookplates by Stephen Gooden, Robert B. Haas Family Arts Library, Yale University.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Stephen Frederick Gooden (1892-1955) was an English engraver, illustrator, and woodcutter. He attended Rugby and the Slade School of Art, served in World War I, and then settled in London. Gooden mastered copperplate engraving, producing book illustrations, banknotes, and numerous bookplates—including several designs for the Royal Family. Although his career was brief, Gooden was a member of the Royal Academy and appointed a Commander of the Most Excellent Order of the British Empire (C.B.E.).
Reference: Dodgson, Campbell. An Iconography of the Engravings of Stephen Gooden . London: E. Mathews ltd., 1944.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection contains bookplates designed by Stephen Gooden (1892-1955). It also includes process materials—e.g., original sketches, trial proofs, and engraved plates—as well as correspondence and ephemera.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three series: I. Bookplates, 1923-1953. II. Process Materials, 1927-1938. III. Correspondence and Ephemera, 1941-1966.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an original preliminary sketch and two final plates (one signed).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes an original pencil drawing and a first-state print.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes versions of the plate before and after lettering.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes examples of both the Edward VIII and the George VI cyphers.

did.unittitle: Lord Harlech (William George Arthur IVth Baron Harlech)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes two different designs, neither of which are in Dodgson.

did.unittitle: Royal College of Surgeons of England

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains the engraved copperplates for several of Gooden's ex-libris designs.

did.unittitle: Hertfordshire Art Society and Elise Fitton

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains various correspondence and ephemera related to Gooden and his ex-libris designs.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes correspondence with collectors and dealers, catalogs and checklists, receipts, and brochures from an exhibition of Stephen Gooden engravings in the Hornby Art Library, Liverpool, July 13-September 29, 1951.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Vol. 28, No. 4 (December 1941) of The Print Collector's Quarterly .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes mats and signage from Yale University Library exhibition of "Engraved Bookplates by Stephen Gooden."
